title: silene scouleri subsp. typica
intro: Silene scouleri subsp. typica, also known as coastal catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Silene scouleri subsp. typica include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe wounds, and promote healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin issues and minor injuries. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and can be used both internally and topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
